samhita: स्तु॒हि श्रु॒त-ङ्ग॑र्त॒सद॑-ययुवा॑न-म्मृ॒गन्न भी॒ममु॑पह॒त्नुमु॒ग्रम् । मृ॒डा ज॑रि॒त्रे रु॑द्र॒ स्तवा॑नो अ॒न्यन्ते॑ अ॒स्मन्निव॑पन्तु॒ सेनाः᳚ ॥
pada: स्तु॒हि । श्रु॒तम् । ग॒र्त॒-सद॑म् । यु॒वा॑नम् । मृ॒गम् । न । भी॒मम् । उ॒प॒-ह॒त्नुम् । उ॒ग्रम् । मृ॒डा । ज॒रि॒त्रे । रु॒द्र॒ । स्तवा॑नः । अ॒न्यम् । ते॒ । अ॒स्मत् । नि । व॒प॒न्त्ु॒ । से॒नाः᳚ ॥
krama: स्तु॒हि श्रु॒तम् । श्रु॒त-ङ्ग॑र्त॒सद॑म् । ग॒र्त॒सद॑-य्युवा॑नम् । यु॒वा॑न-म्मृ॒गम् । मृ॒गन्न । न भी॒मम् । भी॒ममु॑पह॒त्नुम् । उ॒पह॒त्नुमु॒ग्रम् । उ॒ग्र-म्मृ॒डा । मृ॒डा ज॑रि॒त्रे । ज॒रि॒त्रे रु॑द्र । रु॑द्र॒ स्तवा॑नः । स्तवा॑नो॒ अ॒न्यम् । अ॒न्यन्ते॒ । ते अ॒स्मत् । अ॒स्मन्निव॑पन्तु । निव॑पन्तु॒ सेनाः᳚ । सेना॒ इति॑ सेनाः᳚ ॥

sayana.english:
100 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Da. A.]

[Purashcharana for this Mantra]: Having fasted for three nights, one should chant the mantra continuously for three nights. This completes the Purashcharana. This is only for those desiring long life, not others. As the Shruti says: "Man is called a hundred-year-being." Thus, for those whose life is being taken away by deeds causing decay in this or other births, this ritual is for increasing their life. Daily sacrifice 8,000 or 108 offerings of ghee in the fire. Sacrifice 8,000 Durva grasses smeared with curd, honey, and ghee. Daily chant 8,000 times on one's birth star. Specifically, on the birthday, sacrifice 10,000 each of Durva, Apamarga, Shami, and sesame seeds smeared with curd, honey, and ghee. At night, chant 5,000 times and perform circumambulations. A king should sacrifice 10,000 each of Durva, Apamarga, etc., on his birthday; he will be long-lived. For those unable, sacrifice in a flowing stream; for those extremely weak, only chanting. Sacrifice 10,000 offerings of pure rice smeared with honey and ghee; his life increases. Worship the Lord with 10,000 Arka flowers; his prosperity increases. Staying in a temple with a west-facing door, eating only Charu, sleeping on the ground, being celibate, and eating only unsalted food—chant 1,000 times daily near the Lord; he will live for a hundred years in seven births. For the weak, living on alms is recommended. Meditation:

"Meditate on the Lord dancing the Tandava of bliss in front of the Goddess (Devi), focused on the sacrificial fire, brilliant like molten gold."

He states the eighth Rik:

Praise the famous, the one who resides in the heart, the youthful, fierce like a lion, the destroyer, the fierce one. O Rudra, being praised, make us happy in this aging body. Let your armies strike someone other than us. || 8 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: O my speech, praise the famous, well-known Rudra. What kind? 'Gartasadam', always residing in the lotus of the heart. As the Smriti says: "The Lord dwells in the heart of all beings." 'Yuvanam', ever-youthful. 'Upahatnum ugram', having a fierce form to destroy the entire world at the time of dissolution. An example: 'Bhimam mrigam na', like a terrifying lion. Just as a lion is fierce to tear apart an elephant, so is He. O Rudra, being praised by our words, make this aging body—which decays day by day—happy ('mrida'). Let your armies ('senah') strike ('nivapantu') our enemies, not us. || 8 ||

abhinava_shankara.sanskrit:
---
श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।                    १४१
---

भम्' । आयुरैश्वर्यप्रदोऽयं मन्त्रः । त्रिरात्रोपवासपूर्वेकं त्रिरात्रं
निरन्तरजपान्मन्त्रसिद्धिः ॥

    स्तुहि श्रुतं गतेसदं युवानं मृगण्ण भी-
मशुपह्नत्युग्रम् । मृडा जरित्रे रुद्र स्तवा-
नो अन्यन्ते अस्मिन्निषपन्तु सेनाः ॥

    अथाष्टमः— स्तुहीति । अत्र स्वात्मैव संबोध्यते, हे
भात्मन् भगवन्तं स्तुहि; यद्वा हे वचः स्तुहीति । तत्र स्तु-
तिहेतुभूतं पारम्यं दर्शयति— श्रुतिमिति । परमेश्वरत्वेन
सर्वेलोकप्रसिद्धिमित्यर्थः । न चैताटशे कथं मदीयस्तोत्रयुप-
स्थास्यत इव शङ्कध्यम्, यतः गतेसदं गर्वे हृदयकुहरे घीद-
तीति गतेसद्म्, 'ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति'
इति स्मृतेः । न केवलं पारम्यमात्रेण स्तोतव्या, किं तु
दिव्यमङ्गलविग्रहतया-ऋतत्वेनापीत्याह— युवानमिति ।
नित्यतारुण्यसुन्दरिमित्यर्थः । एवं विहितस्य स्तोत्रस्य करणा-
करणयोरिष्टानिष्टप्रापिं दर्शयन्प्रथमसमकरणेऽनिष्टमाह— उप-
हृछुमिति । वर्णाश्रमधर्मान्तुष्ठानेन निज्ञाञातिलक्षिनामभ-
क्तानाघुपहृत्तुम् उपसंहर्तिरिमित्यर्थः । अत्र दष्टान्तमाह—
मृगण्ण भीमामिति । उपमानार्थो नकारः, मृगशिव भीमं
abhinava_shankara.english:
---
Sri Rudra Bhashyam                    141
---

— blazing like refined gold." This mantra grants long life and prosperity. Mantra-siddhi is attained by continuous recitation for three days and nights, preceded by a three-night fast.

    stuhi śrutaṃ gartasadaṃ yuvānaṃ mṛgaṇṇa bhīma-
    mupahṛtyugram | mṛḍā jaritre rudra stavāno
    'nyan te asmin niṣapantu senāḥ ||

    Praise the renowned one, who dwells in the heart-cave, ever youthful,
    who, like a lion, is fearsome and terrible to those who transgress.
    O Rudra, being praised, bring joy (mṛḍa) to one who is aged.
    May your armies fall upon another, not upon us.

Now the eighth mantra — beginning "stuhi" (praise). Here the poet addresses his own self: O my soul (ātman), praise (stuhi) the Lord. Or alternatively: O speech (vacas), praise! The reason why the Lord deserves praise is stated — "śrutam" (the renowned). He is renowned throughout all the worlds as the Supreme Lord (Parameśvara). One need not fear that such a great one would be unapproachable by my praise, because "gartasadam" — he who ever abides in the cave (garta) of the heart — "ī śvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe 'rjuna tiṣṭhati" (The Lord abides in the heart-region of all beings, O Arjuna) — as stated in the Smṛti (sacred scripture). Not only is he to be praised for his supremacy alone, but also for his eternally youthful, beautiful form — thus says "yuvānam" (ever youthful). Having thus established the manner of praise, the poet indicates the undesirable consequence of not praising him — "upahṛtyugram" (the terrible one who withdraws/destroys). Those who disregard the duties of varṇa (social order) and āśrama (stages of life) — the devotees who are known by their defining marks — he withdraws (upasaṃhartum) them. The simile is given — "mṛgaṇṇa bhīmam" (terrible like a lion). The particle "na" (like) indicates comparison: terrible and fearsome like a lion that has risen to tear apart an elephant. And lest one think that even such a great one as the Lord might himself be overpowered by some stronger being — thus eliminating the reason for praise — the text says "ugram" (the Terrible). He is one whom none can overpower.
